summary: Opus 3-tier catalyst sweep (chunk 5 (CLS-DAC)): scan -> deep -> skeptic

survivors_flagged.one_line: Live portfolio breakup: active sale of Life Sciences & Healthcare unit, Morgan Stanley engaged, in active talks with bidders.
Activist (Carronade) + structural fee cut + aggressive buyback (new 14.9M-share auth eff. Mar 6 2026 vs 46.4M out) closing the Foley NAV discount via ongoing asset monetization.
Ohio gas LDC sale ($2.62B to National Fuel) pending HSR/PUCO, expected close Q4 2026 ($1.42B proceeds 2026); real dated divestiture but small vs $20B+ regulated utility.
Definitive Eli Lilly acquisition $38.00 cash + 1 CVR (up to $9.00 on orexin regulatory milestones); shareholder vote Jun 12 2026, close expected Q3 2026 — clean arb spread plus CVR optionality.
Lugano-fraud restatement completed Dec 2025; covenant breach reclassified debt to current — forward milestones: lender amendments + file interim 2025 financials to regain SEC/NYSE compliance.
Two dated bezuclastinib PDUFAs: GIST Nov 30 2026 (priority) and NonAdvSM Dec 30 2026 (no AdCom); SUMMIT/PEAK data already positive so largely de-risked, modest asymmetry.

Dual activist: Ancora coop (Dec 2025) + Sieve Capital proxy (Mar 30 2026) to oust chairman and force strategic-alternatives/sale review; int'l ops ~$1.5B with prior Constellation/CubeCold bids, annual-meeting trigger hardening.

LIVE merger-arb: Angelini Pharma buying Catalyst for $31.50/sh all-cash ($4.1B), announced May-6-2026, board-approved, expected close Q3-2026; Firdapse litigation settled removing a key deal-risk overhang.
Tanbreez rare-earths: dated Jun-26 bulk sample + Aug-26 pilot plant, 15-yr REalloys offtake, Greenland/US-defense supply angle.
Soquelitinib registrational PTCL Phase 3 interim data late-2026; real dated binary but interim-only, small-cap, asymmetry not clearly mispriced.
Holdco deleveraging + Raizen JV impaired to zero/out-of-court restructuring, asset sales & planned subsidiary share distributions; dated but very two-sided (Raizen tail risk).
Dual activists (Third Point + D.E. Shaw) demanding Homes.com alternatives; director nominations + Delaware litigation hardening into the 2026 annual meeting.
Activist Ancora pushing BNSF/CPKC merger amid UP/NS Class I consolidation wave with STB regulatory clock; mgmt won't rule out M&A, but Buffett quashed BNSF talk and it remains speculation not a deal.
Announced Oct-2025 tax-free spin into two public cos (Crop Protection 'New Corteva' + Seed SpinCo), on track for Q4-2026 separation; CEOs named, clean dated spin still ahead.
Aficamten oHCM already approved/launched (fired); forward MAPLE-HCM sNDA PDUFA Nov-14-2026 is a dated binary but lower-asymmetry label expansion, far out.

verified.catalyst: Clarivate is running an active, advisor-led (Morgan Stanley) sale of its ~$390M-revenue Life Sciences & Healthcare segment, with proceeds earmarked to cut its ~$4.5B debt against a $1.5B market cap.
verified.dated_milestone: No signed deal or announced close date; next catalyst is a process update at Q2 2026 earnings (~late July/early Aug 2026), or an interim sale announcement (indeterminate).
verified.instrument: equity / long-dated calls (LEAPS) — small, deep-value/event-driven; binary on a deal materializing
verified.bloom_gates: G3 (concrete unpriced figure: $389.8M LS&H 2025 revenue; deleveraging optionality not priced in — stock $2.41 near 52wk low $1.66, below 50d $2.54 / 200d $3.13). Soft G2 (management publicly committed to the sale process + proceeds-to-deleverage). FAILS G1 — no named counterparty; Morgan Stanley is advisor only, IQVIA/Norstella/PE are press speculation.
verified.edge: M — real forced-divestiture with disclosed financials and a depressed, un-re-rated equity (deleveraging optionality genuinely unpriced), but no named buyer and no dated/binding milestone caps conviction; deal-or-no-deal is binary with no committed timeline.
verified.primary_source: Clarivate FY2025 8-K/earnings release (SEC EDGAR, filed Feb 24-25, 2026) confirming Morgan Stanley-advised LS&H sale process; Q1 2026 results (Apr 29, 2026) reaffirming process; FMP quote 2026-06-06 ($2.41, -3.2%).
verified.analysis: The breakup is genuine and idiosyncratic: confirmed Feb 24-25, 2026 alongside FY2025 results, Morgan Stanley engaged, "active discussions with interested parties" reaffirmed, and the stock is NOT re-rated (trading $2.41 near 52-week lows, below all moving averages) so the deleveraging optionality on the ~$390M-revenue unit vs $1.5B cap / $4.5B debt remains unpriced (clears G3). But it fails strict ACTIVE gating: no named counterparty (G1 fails — only an advisor and press-speculated acquirers) and, critically, no dated milestone — there is no signed LOI, binding agreement, or announced close date, only an open-ended process. That combination — real + forward + unpriced but soft-dated and counterparty-less — lands it at WATCH/5; re-rate to ACTIVE on a signed transaction, named bidder, or a hard process deadline.

verified.catalyst: Cannae is grinding closed a ~41% discount to its own published $24.31 SOTP NAV (vs $14.44 price) via a new 14.9M-share buyback authorization (~32% of 46.4M shares out) funded by ongoing non-core asset monetization, post the eliminated external management fee.
verified.dated_milestone: 14.9M-share buyback auth effective Mar 6 2026 (ongoing execution); next hard date = Q2 2026 earnings / 10-Q (~early Aug 2026) for buyback pace + asset-sale proceeds; zero-debt 2026 target outstanding
verified.instrument: stock (equity; LEAPS viable given multi-quarter discount-closure timeline, but wide bid/ask and slow grind favor common)
verified.bloom_gates: G2 (dated 14.9M-share buyback auth, Mar 6 2026, ~32% of float, asset-sale-funded) + G3 (company SOTP NAV $24.31/sh vs $14.44 price = ~41% unpriced discount, Feb 2026); G1 partially met but stale (Carronade named but proxy fight concluded Dec 12 2025)
verified.edge: M — discount and dated buyback are real and large, but the "Foley discount" is historically sticky and closure is a slow multi-quarter grind, not a discrete event; activist + fee-cut legs already fired/re-rated.
verified.primary_source: StockTitan / SEC 8-K, Cannae buyback increase to 14.9M shares, dated Mar 6 2026; SOTP NAV $24.31 from Cannae Feb 2026 disclosure (Jax Daily Record, Apr 30 2026); price $14.44 (FMP quote, Jun 6 2026)
verified.kill_fact: All load-bearing facts verified against primary/credible sources. Buyback: Board authorized 14.9M shares (10M new + 4.9M remaining) effective Mar 6 2026, explicitly funded "as the Company generates liquidity through the sale of non-core assets," vs 46.4M shares out (~32%) (StockTitan/Yahoo/SEC). NAV: company-published illustrative non-GAAP SOTP NAV ~$24.31/sh as of Feb 23 2026; live quote price $14.44 (exact match) = ~41% discount. Activist leg correctly FIRED: Carronade won 2 of 4 board seats at Dec 12 2025 annual meeting (Aboelnaga, Schaible) (Nasdaq/Investing.com/Olshan); external mgmt fee already eliminated. 200-day avg $15.38, price near it. Caveat (not disqualifying): $24.31 is mgmt's own illustrative figure (assumes 21% tax on unrealized gains), explicitly disclaimed as not a price target, and there is no single hard near-term catalyst date — the driver is the buyback/monetization grind, which legitimately caps conviction at 6/ACTIVE. Line is forward, dated, idiosyncratic, and tradeable in common; LEAPS framing reasonable given multi-quarter timeline.
verified.analysis: Verified: Board authorized 14.9M-share buyback (10M new + 4.9M remaining) on Mar 6 2026 vs 46.4M shares out, explicitly to be funded "as the Company generates liquidity through the sale of non-core assets," against a company-published Feb 2026 SOTP NAV of $24.31/sh while the stock sits at $14.44 (~41% discount, near 200-day avg). The activist leg is FIRED — Carronade won 2 of 4 board seats at the Dec 12 2025 annual meeting — and the external management fee was already eliminated, so the live forward driver is the buyback + asset-monetization cadence rather than a discrete pending event. Real, dated, idiosyncratic (not macro) and forward on the capital-return mechanics, but lacks a single hard near-term catalyst date, capping conviction at 6/ACTIVE.

verified.catalyst: CenterPoint is selling its Ohio natural gas LDC (Vectren Energy Delivery of Ohio; ~335k customers, 5,900 miles pipeline) to National Fuel Gas for $2.62B, expecting $1.42B cash proceeds in 2026 plus a $1.20B seller note in 2027.
verified.dated_milestone: Expected close Q4 2026; next gate is PUCO notice-filing review (filed Jan 9, 2026, targeted complete by end of Q3 FY26). HSR already satisfied (30-day waiting period expired after Jan 16, 2026 filing).
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparty: National Fuel Gas Co) + G3 (specific figures: $2.62B price, $1.42B 2026 cash, $1.20B 2027 seller note, ~1.9x rate-base multiple)
verified.edge: L — deal signed Oct 20, 2025 (~8 months old, well-telegraphed); proceeds (~$1.42B 2026 cash) are ~5% of $27.9B mkt cap and small vs ~$65.5B 10-yr capex base; redeployment into Texas capex already baked into reaffirmed 2026 non-GAAP EPS guide ($1.89-$1.91, 8% growth), so largely priced. Remaining PUCO step is procedural/low-drama. CNP otherwise trades as a rate-sensitive regulated utility (macro).
verified.primary_source: CenterPoint/National Fuel press release & Businesswire, Oct 20-21, 2025 ($2.62B sale terms); National Fuel 8-K filings (PUCO notice filed Jan 9, 2026; HSR filed Jan 16, 2026, waiting period expired); CenterPoint Q1 2026 earnings call, Apr 23, 2026 (Q4 2026 close reaffirmed, guidance $1.89-$1.91).
verified.analysis: The divestiture is real, specific, dated, and still forward (not closed as of the Apr 23, 2026 Q1 call, which reaffirmed a Q4 2026 close), clearing Bloom G1 and G3. But the edge is low: it was announced ~8 months ago, the cash is modest relative to CenterPoint's $27.9B cap and large regulated capex program, and management has already folded the capital recycling into reaffirmed guidance — so it is a known, largely-priced story rather than an unpriced re-rating. The only live gate (PUCO notice review) is routine; a clean close confirmation or any unexpected PUCO friction in Q3-Q4 2026 would be the actionable moments to watch.

verified.catalyst: Definitive Eli Lilly acquisition at $38.00 cash + non-transferable CVR up to $9.00 (FDA-approval milestones for cleminorexton/ORX142 in narcolepsy/idiopathic hypersomnia), via UK scheme of arrangement.
verified.dated_milestone: Shareholder vote Jun 12 2026; UK court sanction + antitrust to follow; outside date Sep 30 2026 (close expected Q3 2026).
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparty Eli Lilly) + G2 (signed definitive merger agreement / DEFM14A); G3 partial — CVR up-to-$9.00 is a specific figure but is NOT currently unpriced (market already embeds it).
verified.edge: L — no positive cash arb: CNTA $39.62 trades $1.62 ABOVE the $38.00 cash floor, so deal completion alone locks a loss; residual upside is purely the CVR, and ~$1.62 of the $9.00 is already priced. Remaining edge is a multi-year binary clinical/regulatory bet, not a clean 5-week spread.
verified.primary_source: CNTA DEFM14A (SEC, filed FY2026, sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/0001847903/...d148719ddefm14a.htm) + Lilly/Centessa press release (Jun 2026); FMP quote CNTA $39.62 @ 2026-06-06.
verified.analysis: The merger is real, definitive, and dated (Lilly/$38 cash + CVR, vote Jun 12, outside date Sep 30 2026), so G1/G2 pass and it is forward/not-fired. But the triage thesis of a "clean arb spread" is false: at $39.62 the stock is already $1.62 over the $38 cash consideration, meaning the market is pricing the CVR and there is negative cash carry — completion guarantees a loss absent CVR payouts that hinge on future FDA approvals years out. That converts the trade from a tight merger-arb into a speculative CVR/biotech bet with low, non-arb edge, so WATCH not ACTIVE.

verified.catalyst: Lugano-fraud restatement and the path back to SEC/NYSE compliance — but every flagged milestone has already been completed, so there is no remaining forward catalyst.
verified.dated_milestone: All milestones FIRED, none forward: restatement completed Dec 8 2025; credit-facility amendment Dec 19 2025; delinquent 10-Qs filed Q1 Dec 18 / Q2 Dec 29 2025 / Q3 Jan 14 2026; FY25 10-K Feb 27 2026; on-time Q1'26 10-Q May 6 2026; residual Section 302 governance cured via annual meeting held May 21 2026. Next scheduled date: none catalyst-relevant (Q2'26 10-Q ~early Aug 2026, routine).
verified.instrument: none (stock only; catalyst spent — no asymmetric forward setup)
verified.bloom_gates: None pass as FORWARD. G2/G3 (concrete commitment / unpriced compliance figure) existed but are all in the past tense — restatement, amendment, and 10-Q/10-K filings are completed and the re-rate (52wk low $4.58 → $10.64, ~2.3x) is already in the price.
verified.edge: L — thesis is fully fired; no dated forward milestone left for this lane, and the deep-value compliance re-rate has already occurred. Residual upside (ADW activist liquidation-at-$26, Sterno $292.5M deleveraging) is a separate, contested thesis, not a dated company commitment.
verified.primary_source: FMP SEC filings (10-Q Q1'26 filed 2026-05-06; FY25 10-K 2026-02-27; Q3'25 10-Q 2026-01-14) + GlobeNewswire press releases: "Completes Restatement" (2025-12-08) and "Amendment to Existing Credit Facility" (2025-12-19); NYSE current-filer confirmation per Q3'25 earnings release (2026-01-14).
verified.analysis: CODI = Compass Diversified. The triage thesis is stale: the Lugano restatement (Dec 8 2025), senior-secured credit-facility amendment (Dec 19 2025), and all three delinquent 2025 10-Qs were completed by mid-Jan 2026, the FY25 10-K filed Feb 27 2026, and the Q1'26 10-Q filed on time May 6 2026 — so CODI is already a current filer in compliance with its reporting covenants, and the lone residual NYSE item (Section 302, missed 2025 annual meeting) was cured by the May 21 2026 annual meeting. There is no remaining dated, forward compliance/restructuring catalyst, and the distressed re-rate is already reflected (year low $4.58 vs current $10.64). What is live now — ADW Capital's 7.65% activist push for an orderly liquidation at ~$26/share and active deleveraging from the $292.5M Sterno divestiture — is a different, contested thesis rather than the flagged catalyst, so this fails the forward/not-fired gate.

verified.catalyst: Two dated bezuclastinib FDA decisions: GIST (bezu+sunitinib, 2L post-imatinib) PDUFA Nov 30 2026 under Priority Review/RTOR with no AdCom planned, and NonAdvSM PDUFA Dec 30 2026.
verified.dated_milestone: GIST PDUFA Nov 30 2026 (priority); NonAdvSM PDUFA Dec 30 2026 — next: GIST decision Nov 30 2026
verified.instrument: equity (COGT ~$36.5); Nov/Dec 2026 calls to span both PDUFAs if playing the binary
verified.bloom_gates: G2 (concrete committed FDA action with assigned PDUFA dates); partial G3 (specific dated decisions, but approval already largely priced so not a clearly unpriced figure)
verified.edge: L — Phase 3 PEAK data (PFS 16.5 vs 9.2mo, HR 0.50) already public/positive; Priority Review, RTOR, Breakthrough, no AdCom and no FDA review issues flagged make approval the consensus base case. Sell-side already Strong Buy (avg PT ~$54 vs ~$36.5), so dates are confirmatory, not asymmetric; residual edge is launch execution/label, not the binary.
verified.primary_source: Cogent Biosciences press releases via GlobeNewswire — GIST NDA acceptance/Priority Review PDUFA Nov 30 2026 (May 28 2026) and NonAdvSM NDA acceptance PDUFA Dec 30 2026 (Mar 16 2026); PEAK ASCO data (May 30 2026)
verified.analysis: Both PDUFAs are real, named (FDA), and firmly dated (GIST Nov 30 2026 priority/no AdCom; NonAdvSM Dec 30 2026), and the events are forward (not fired). But the approval binary is heavily de-risked by public, statistically strong PEAK Phase 3 data and the absence of any flagged review issues or AdCom, so the upside is largely already re-rated into the stock and consensus. This is a genuine dated forward catalyst with modest, mostly-priced asymmetry — WATCH-grade, not ACTIVE.

verified.catalyst: Dual-activist (Ancora + Sieve Capital) push to oust Chairman Patterson and force a strategic-alternatives/international-sale review at Americold Realty Trust.
verified.dated_milestone: Primary trigger (2026 Annual Meeting) already occurred May 18, 2026; only residual dated event is the EQT US-asset JV close, expected Q3 2026 (separate deleveraging deal, not the activist sale thesis).
verified.bloom_gates: G1 named counterparties present (Sieve Capital, Ancora; Constellation Cold/CubeCold for ~$1.5B int'l ops) — but FORWARD test fails: trigger fired/re-rated.
verified.edge: L — well-covered, fully public, and the binary event has already resolved against the activists; no informational/timing edge remains.
verified.primary_source: StockTitan 8-K recap of COLD 2026 Annual Meeting results (May 18, 2026); Minichart/COLD investor update + StockTitan on EQT JV (June 1, 2026).
verified.analysis: The annual-meeting trigger that anchored this thesis has FIRED: at the May 18, 2026 meeting all ten directors including Chairman Patterson and Andy Power were re-elected, defeating Sieve Capital's withhold campaign despite Glass Lewis opposition, and Ancora had already settled earlier in 2026 for two board seats. Rather than pursuing the demanded ~$1.5B international sale to Constellation/CubeCold, the company on June 1, 2026 announced an EQT JV over 12 US properties (~$1.15B proceeds, closing Q3 2026) for deleveraging, sidestepping the activist sale path; the international divestiture is no longer in the company's narrative. No real dated FORWARD activist/sale catalyst remains, so this gates to NONE — the only upcoming dated event (EQT JV close) is a separate, already-disclosed balance-sheet transaction, not the activist trigger.

verified.catalyst: Angelini Pharma is acquiring Catalyst for $31.50/sh all-cash ($4.1B), board-approved definitive agreement signed May-7-2026, expected to close Q3-2026.
verified.dated_milestone: Special meeting stockholder vote + HSR antitrust clearance; deal expected to close Q3-2026 (next: PREM14A filed, DEFM14A/meeting date pending)
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparty: Angelini Pharma) + G2 (concrete commitment: $31.50/sh all-cash, no financing condition); fails G3 (no currently-unpriced figure — $31.50 cap already captured)
verified.edge: L — gross spread only $0.23 (CPRX $31.27 vs $31.50 deal); ~0.73% gross to a Q3-2026 close = standard arb carry, already re-rated.
verified.primary_source: Catalyst 8-K / Angelini Pharma press release, May-7-2026 ($31.50/sh, $4.1B, Q3-2026 close); FMP quote CPRX $31.27 (2026-06-05 close); Firdapse-Hetero settlement 8-K May-7-2026
verified.analysis: Real, dated, board-approved all-cash merger with named acquirer (Angelini) and the Firdapse/Hetero patent suit settled May-7-2026 (no generic before Jan-2035), removing the key deal-risk overhang. But the catalyst has already FIRED and is fully priced: CPRX trades at $31.27 vs the $31.50 deal price, a mere ~0.73% gross spread, so the announcement upside is captured and only standard merger-arb carry remains. No unpriced forward figure (fails G3); residual edge is low and the stock will not re-rate further absent a topping bid, so WATCH/score 3 rather than ACTIVE.

verified.catalyst: Tanbreez (Greenland) HREE project: 15-yr binding REalloys offtake for 15% of Phase-1 output (Dy/Tb priority) plus Jun-26 bulk-sample mobilization and Aug-26 pilot-plant Stage-1 build.
verified.dated_milestone: Pilot-plant Stage-1 head office/warehouse completion targeted Aug-2026 (infrastructure, not production); bulk-sample field crews mobilized Jun-1-2026 (no stated tonnage/first-concentrate date). Next dated: Aug-2026.
verified.instrument: equity (high-beta de-SPAC; long shares only — no dated binary for a defined-risk call)
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparty REalloys/ALOY) + G2 (concrete 15% Phase-1 offtake commitment) — but both attach to the FIRED May-21-2026 signing; G3 fails (no specific currently-unpriced forward figure: no dated first-concentrate or first-revenue).
verified.edge: L — the gate-passing facts (binding 15-yr offtake, 92.5% ownership approval) are already public and already re-rated; remaining forward dates are infrastructure footings, not value events.
verified.primary_source: criticalmetalscorp.com / globenewswire — "Project Update on Acceleration Plan and Tanbreez Development Progress" (Jun-2-2026) and "15-Year Binding Definitive Off-Take Agreement with REalloys" (May-21-2026); quote via FMP (Jun-6-2026, $9.74).
verified.analysis: The triage's strongest items — the 15-year binding REalloys offtake (15% of Tanbreez Phase-1, Dy/Tb priority) and the 92.5% ownership grant — were announced May 21 / Apr 17 2026 and have already FIRED and re-rated; CRML is $9.74 today (down 11% on the day) versus a $1.32 yr-low, i.e. the supply/defense thesis is largely in the tape. The only genuinely forward dated milestone is an Aug-2026 pilot-plant Stage-1 infrastructure completion (footings underway), which is readiness, not first-concentrate or first-revenue, so it fails G3 (no specific unpriced figure) and is not a discrete catalyst. The residual "Greenland/US-defense rare-earth supply" framing is a macro/thematic bet, which the gate excludes.

verified.catalyst: Interim efficacy/futility data from the registrational Phase 3 soquelitinib trial (NCT06561048) in relapsed/refractory PTCL — soquelitinib vs physician's choice (belinostat/pralatrexate), 150 pts, PFS primary endpoint.
verified.dated_milestone: Phase 3 PTCL interim data, company-guided to late 2026 (full trial completion 2027). Next date: ~Q4 2026 interim readout.
verified.instrument: equity (CRVS); listed options exist but thin — directional equity or long-dated calls into Q4-2026 if sizing the binary
verified.bloom_gates: G2 partial — a company-committed registrational milestone with a defined PFS endpoint and guided date, but a self-imposed clinical timeline, not a third-party contractual commitment. Fails G1 (no named counterparty/deal) and G3 (no specific currently-unpriced figure; asymmetry not clearly dislocated).
verified.edge: L — real dated idiosyncratic binary, but (1) it is an INTERIM look, not the topline registrational readout (definitive event is 2027), so a clean fire/re-rate is not assured; (2) ~$935M mkt cap for a single-asset clinical-stage name is not an obviously mispriced EV, and 52-wk range $3.55–$26.95 shows the binary is already richly embedded in IV/price; (3) only Phase 1/1b 6.2-mo mPFS supports the bet. Triage's "asymmetry not clearly mispriced" holds.
verified.primary_source: https://corvuspharma.gcs-web.com/news-releases/news-release-details/corvus-pharmaceuticals-initiates-registrational-phase-3-clinical (Corvus Phase 3 initiation PR); Phase 1/1b final data PR 2025-12-08; FMP quote dated 2026-06-06.
verified.analysis: Confirmed: Corvus is running registrational Phase 3 NCT06561048 (soquelitinib vs belinostat/pralatrexate, r/r PTCL, n=150, PFS endpoint, FDA Orphan Drug + Fast Track), with management guiding interim data to late 2026 and completion to 2027 — so this is a genuine dated, forward, idiosyncratic (non-macro) binary that has not fired. It falls short of ACTIVE because the catalyst is an interim analysis rather than the definitive topline (the unambiguous re-rate event is 2027), it clears only a partial G2 (no named counterparty, no specific unpriced figure), and at a ~$935M market cap with a $3.55–$26.95 52-week range the asymmetry is not clearly mispriced. Correct disposition is WATCH/score 5: track the binary into Q4 2026 but the edge is low because the interim may be inconclusive and the optionality is already embedded.

verified.catalyst: Holdco deleveraging via subsidiary stake sales and planned eventual share distributions to shareholders, with a Raizen JV (impaired to zero) stake sale as two-sided optionality.
verified.dated_milestone: No discrete dated forward catalyst; next event is Q2 2026 earnings (~mid-Aug 2026, reporting only). Compass over-allotment greenshoe window expires ~Jun 6, 2026.
verified.bloom_gates: G1/G2/G3 all present historically (Compass buyers, R$6.2bn debt redemption, Raizen zero-mark) but every one is already FIRED and re-rated; no gate is simultaneously dated AND forward AND unpriced.
verified.edge: L — the concrete, dated legs (Compass offering priced May 7 2026, debt redemptions, Raizen write-down) are spent and the stock is already at a 52-wk low (-55% YTD); remaining upside is undated/structural.
verified.primary_source: Cosan Q1 2026 earnings call summary (ainvest.com, ~May 26 2026) + Compass secondary pricing 6-K/TipRanks (priced May 7 2026); FMP quote $2.79, 52wk $2.73-$6.22 (Jun 6 2026)
verified.analysis: Every specific, dated leg of the deleveraging thesis has already fired: Compass secondary priced May 7 2026 (BRL2.15bn, stake 88%->77.25%), R$6.2bn of notes redeemed (2029 called Feb 17 2026), and Raizen marked to zero (Dec 31 2025) with equity-method recognition ceased Mar 31 2026 — all reflected in CSAN's ~55% YTD drawdown to $2.79. The genuinely forward items (Raizen stake disposal, subsidiary share distributions, holdco dissolution) are explicitly undated ("may sell in the future," dissolution "within 3-5 years") and the Raizen tail is real but two-sided and uncatalyzed. Fails the strict dated-forward gate: no clean G1/G2/G3 that is unfired, dated, and unpriced.

verified.catalyst: The "dual-activist proxy fight into the 2026 annual meeting" thesis has collapsed: Third Point exited its entire CSGP position and abandoned the campaign (Apr 12, 2026), while D.E. Shaw is bound by a Support Agreement/standstill, leaving the June 23, 2026 annual meeting uncontested.
verified.dated_milestone: 2026 Annual Meeting of Stockholders: Tue, June 23, 2026, 10:00 a.m. ET — but uncontested (no dissident slate), so not a tradable catalyst.
verified.instrument: none (no forward catalyst; if any, common equity only — no defined event to express)
verified.bloom_gates: None pass forward. G1 (named counterparties Third Point/D.E. Shaw) exist historically but Third Point is gone and D.E. Shaw is settled into a standstill, so no live counterparty is pursuing action. G2/G3 fail: no concrete forward commitment and the $10-11B "value destroyed" figure is a backward critique, not an unpriced forward figure with a date.
verified.edge: L — situation is widely covered (CNBC, Reuters, SEC filings) and the resolution (Third Point exit, D.E. Shaw standstill) is public and already priced; the 6% activist pop already fired in late Jan 2026.
verified.primary_source: CNBC, "Activist Dan Loeb dusts off his poison pen... CoStar" (Jan 31, 2026) + Reuters/TipRanks "Third Point won't run proxy fight at CoStar, exits position" (Apr 12, 2026) + CoStar DEF 14A FY2026 (SEC EDGAR, accession 000119312526197205; meeting June 23, 2026) + DEFA14A on Feb 13, 2026 severance amendment.
verified.analysis: The triage premise is stale/fired: Third Point publicly exited its entire stake and declined a proxy fight on Apr 12, 2026 ("disposed of our position in its entirety"), and D.E. Shaw signed a Support Agreement with customary standstill/voting provisions (no nominations, no proposals, no board-member removal, ownership capped). The Delaware severance-plan litigation was defused when CoStar amended the plan to remove the director clause on Feb 13, 2026. Net: the June 23 meeting is uncontested, the activist pop already happened, and there is no live named counterparty driving a dated, unpriced forward event — fails FORWARD and Bloom gates, so NONE.

verified.catalyst: Ancora is pushing CSX to seek a BNSF/CPKC merger amid the Class I consolidation wave, but the named counterparties (BNSF/Buffett, CPKC) have publicly declined and the activist's deal-or-fire-CEO demand was already resolved by Hinrichs's ouster.
verified.dated_milestone: No CSX-deal milestone. Only adjacent dated event is the UP-NS STB supplemental-info deadline July 27, 2026 (CSX is an opposing intervenor, not a target); STB final decision late-2026/2027.
verified.bloom_gates: none (G1 fails: named counterparties BNSF/CPKC explicitly declined, not willing; G2 fails: no concrete CSX commitment/process announced; G3 fails: no specific currently-unpriced CSX deal figure)
verified.edge: L — thesis is well-worn since Aug 2025 and already re-rated; CSX +49% YoY, $46.99 near 52-wk high ($47.21) vs $38 200-day avg, so consolidation optionality is largely priced; multiple DCFs peg intrinsic ~$31.
verified.primary_source: Reuters/Yahoo Finance "Buffett shoots down talk of Berkshire railroad merger, CSX shares slide" (Aug 2025); FreightWaves "Buffett: Berkshire will not bid for CSX or Norfolk Southern"; SourcingJournal STB UP-NS supplemental-data coverage and CSX CEO Steve Angel opposition at May 16, 2026 annual meeting; FMP quote CSX $46.99 (2026-06-06).
verified.analysis: The Ancora catalyst is ~10-month-old speculation, not a live forward deal: BNSF (Buffett) and CPKC have publicly said they aren't interested, CSX shares already slid on Buffett's denial, and the activist's CEO demand was met when Hinrichs was replaced by M&A-pedigreed Steve Angel in Sept 2025. The only currently-dated event is CSX acting as a defensive intervenor against the rival UP-NS merger (STB supplemental deadline Jul 27, 2026), which is a regulatory/macro-industry dynamic rather than a CSX-specific, named-counterparty, committed transaction. With the stock near its 52-week high and consolidation optionality embedded, there is no real, dated, forward, unpriced CSX-deal catalyst — it fails all three Bloom gates.

verified.catalyst: Corteva will execute a board-approved, tax-free spin into two separate NYSE-listed public companies — New Corteva (crop protection, ~$7.8B 2025 sales) and SpinCo (Pioneer seed, ~$9.9B 2025 sales) — crystallizing a sum-of-parts re-rating.
verified.dated_milestone: Form 10 public filing expected late Q2 2026; Investor Day Sept 15, 2026 at NYSE; separation completion H2/Q4 2026.
verified.instrument: stock (or long-dated call into Q3/Q4-2026 separation); pure-play SOTP unlock favors common.
verified.bloom_gates: G2 (concrete board-approved commitment with definitive timeline) + partial G3 (segment financials $9.9B seed / $7.8B crop disclosed for SOTP); G1 satisfied via named entities/CEOs (Luke Kissam New Corteva, Chuck Magro SpinCo) rather than a merger counterparty.
verified.edge: M — clean dated catalyst with concrete near-term milestones (Form 10 late Q2, Investor Day Sep 15), but announced Oct-2025 so partially telegraphed; stock at $77 sits below 50d ($81.25) and ~10% off year-high ($85.63), indicating the actual separation value-unlock is not yet fully priced.
verified.primary_source: Corteva Q1-2026 8-K/earnings release (filed ~May 2026, SEC EDGAR CIK 1755672) detailing separation milestones incl. late-Q2 Form 10 + Sep 15, 2026 Investor Day; original separation plan announced Oct 1, 2025; CEO Luke Kissam named for New Corteva (joined June 1, 2026).
verified.kill_fact: Forward catalyst is REAL and UNFIRED: Corteva's board-approved tax-free spin into New Corteva (crop protection) + the seed/Pioneer SpinCo — now branded VYLOR, Inc. (line omits this) — is reaffirmed on-track for Q4 2026 (Q1 call, May 5 2026: 'on-track for 4Q 2026 separation'), with Investor Day Sep 15 2026 at NYSE confirmed exactly, CEO Luke Kissam (New Corteva) named, and Form 10 already initially filed. Live $77.03 (off $85.63 high) confirmed. The distribution event is still ahead and price is DOWN not re-rated — not fired. CORRECTION: '50d/200d-converged' is false — 50d $81.25 vs 200d $72.62 (~$8.6 gap); price sits below 50d but ABOVE 200d. Edge is medium (announcement priced; remaining edge = execution + SOTP disclosure), as the line itself concedes.
verified.analysis: Real, specific, dated and forward: a board-approved tax-free 2-way spin (New Corteva crop protection + Pioneer Seed SpinCo) on track for H2/Q4-2026 with named CEOs and a concrete milestone chain — Form 10 filing late Q2-2026, credit-agency review, then Investor Day Sep 15, 2026 at NYSE and final capital structures in H2. Not a macro bet; it is a self-help structural separation whose value-crystallizing event (the distribution) is still ahead, and price action ($77, below 50d/200d-converged, off the $85.63 high) suggests it is not fired/fully re-rated. Caveat: announced ~8 months ago so the announcement pop is gone — the edge is the execution path (Form 10, Investor Day, segment-level disclosure tightening the SOTP), not fresh surprise, hence Medium edge and score 7.

verified.catalyst: FDA PDUFA decision on the MAPLE-HCM sNDA expanding aficamten (MYQORZO) to first-line monotherapy positioning vs metoprolol in obstructive HCM.
verified.dated_milestone: MAPLE-HCM sNDA PDUFA date Nov 14, 2026 (FDA accepted for filing; confirmed in Q1 2026 update / 8-K).
verified.instrument: stock (CYTK); options exist but the catalyst is a slow-burn regulatory label expansion, not a sharp data drop.
verified.bloom_gates: Closest to G3 (dated regulatory decision), but no specific currently-unpriced figure; no G1 named counterparty or G2 concrete commitment.
verified.edge: L — pivotal MAPLE-HCM topline already read positively (May 2025) and ESC data presented May 2026, so the binary is largely de-risked; base oHCM approval already fired/launched; PDUFA ~5mo out; name richly valued ($71.5 vs $80.2 52wk-high, $8.9B cap, fresh $805M raise) and heavily covered.
verified.primary_source: Cytokinetics Q1 2026 results / 8-K (GlobeNewsWire, May 5, 2026) confirming MAPLE-HCM sNDA accepted with PDUFA Nov 14, 2026; EU launch PR (June 1, 2026).
verified.analysis: The headline aficamten/MYQORZO oHCM approval and commercial launch are already FIRED (US live with ~680 pts in Q1 2026; EU launched in Germany June 1, 2026), so the forward item is solely the MAPLE-HCM sNDA, a real DATED binary with a Nov-14-2026 PDUFA. However it is a low-asymmetry label expansion: the drug is already approved and selling, the pivotal data is public and positive (de-risked), and approval mainly strengthens first-line monotherapy positioning rather than opening a net-new market. It clears the floor for a dated forward catalyst (so not NONE) but lacks a concrete unpriced figure or named counterparty and is ~5 months out into a richly-valued, well-covered name, supporting WATCH.
